speaker: TIMON
text: Let me look back upon thee. O thou wall

speaker: TIMON
text: That girdles in those wolves, dive in the earth

speaker: TIMON
text: And fence not Athens! Matrons, turn incontinent!

speaker: TIMON
text: Obedience fail in children! Slaves and fools,

speaker: TIMON
text: Pluck the grave wrinkled Senate from the bench

speaker: TIMON
text: And minister in their steads! To general filths

speaker: TIMON
text: Convert o’ th’ instant, green virginity!

speaker: TIMON
text: Do ’t in your parents’ eyes! Bankrupts, hold fast!

speaker: TIMON
text: Rather than render back, out with your knives

speaker: TIMON
text: And cut your trusters’ throats! Bound servants, steal!

speaker: TIMON
text: Large-handed robbers your grave masters are,

speaker: TIMON
text: And pill by law. Maid, to thy master’s bed!

speaker: TIMON
text: Thy mistress is o’ th’ brothel. Son of sixteen,

speaker: TIMON
text: Pluck the lined crutch from thy old limping sire;

speaker: TIMON
text: With it beat out his brains! Piety and fear,

speaker: TIMON
text: Religion to the gods, peace, justice, truth,

speaker: TIMON
text: Domestic awe, night rest, and neighborhood,

speaker: TIMON
text: Instruction, manners, mysteries, and trades,

speaker: TIMON
text: Degrees, observances, customs, and laws,

speaker: TIMON
text: Decline to your confounding contraries,

speaker: TIMON
text: And yet confusion live! Plagues incident to men,

speaker: TIMON
text: Your potent and infectious fevers heap

speaker: TIMON
text: On Athens, ripe for stroke! Thou cold sciatica,

speaker: TIMON
text: Cripple our senators, that their limbs may halt

speaker: TIMON
text: As lamely as their manners! Lust and liberty,

speaker: TIMON
text: Creep in the minds and marrows of our youth,

speaker: TIMON
text: That ’gainst the stream of virtue they may strive

speaker: TIMON
text: And drown themselves in riot! Itches, blains,

speaker: TIMON
text: Sow all th’ Athenian bosoms, and their crop

speaker: TIMON
text: Be general leprosy! Breath infect breath,

speaker: TIMON
text: That their society, as their friendship, may

speaker: TIMON
text: Be merely poison! Nothing I’ll bear from thee

speaker: TIMON
text: But nakedness, thou detestable town!

speaker: TIMON
text: Take thou that too, with multiplying bans!

speaker: TIMON
text: Timon will to the woods, where he shall find

speaker: TIMON
text: Th’ unkindest beast more kinder than mankind.

speaker: TIMON
text: The gods confound—hear me, you good gods all!—

speaker: TIMON
text: Th’ Athenians both within and out that wall,

speaker: TIMON
text: And grant, as Timon grows, his hate may grow

speaker: TIMON
text: To the whole race of mankind, high and low!

speaker: TIMON
text: Amen.
